factors.name: Hot Jobs Report (NFP +172K vs +85K expected)
factors.summary: May NFP beat expectations significantly (172K vs 85K consensus), with prior 3 months revised upward. This crushes Fed rate-cut narrative and pushes yields higher, directly compressing long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Treasury Yield Spike
factors.summary: 10Y yields surged 6bp and 2Y yields jumped 12bp on Friday following NFP. Higher real yields directly pressure Nasdaq growth stocks with extended valuations; move has already cascaded into equities.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Repricing
factors.summary: Bond market pricing now reflects expectation of a 25bp rate hike in 2026 per KPMG Chief Economist. This represents a hawkish pivot away from the pre-NFP dovish bias, killing momentum rally thesis.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Equity Raises (GOOG, META, MSFT)
factors.summary: Google sold $85B equity this week; Meta reportedly considering tens of billions in dilutive capital raise for AI capex. Microsoft may also tap equity markets. Supply overhang and dilution concerns weigh on sentiment.

factors.name: Chip Sector Rout (NVDA, Marvell, Micron crashes)
factors.summary: Semiconductor stocks posted worst day in 6 years on Friday. Micron and Marvell tumbled despite Marvell being added to S&P 500. Rotation out of AI/chip momentum is broad and conviction-driven.

factors.name: Nasdaq Already Down 4.18% in Single Session
factors.summary: NAS100 posted worst day since April 2025 on Friday with -1,121 point drop (largest on record). At intraday low of 25,753, the index saw 3.84% decline. Extended move is already in progress; late to add shorts.

factors.name: Broader Risk-Off + Crypto Weakness (BTC sub-$60K)
factors.summary: Bitcoin crashed below $60K for first time since October 2024; $200B wiped from crypto market. Rising correlation between NQ and risk assets amplifies tech selloff; flight to safety is real.

factors.name: Valuation Compression Risk Intact
factors.summary: Tech multiples are elevated; real yields moving higher (TIPS sensitivity) and nominal yields rising compress long-duration growth. No catalyst in immediate term to re-inflate multiple expansion.

factors.name: Goldman Sachs Bull Case (S&P 500 to 8,000)
factors.summary: John Flood (Goldman) called Friday's pullback a buying opportunity with clear path to S&P 500 8,000 in 2026. Some institutional conviction still present, but retail sentiment appears broken.

factors.name: Earnings Growth Still Expected
factors.summary: Goldman noted continued earnings growth is critical for market momentum, and corporate profits remain at record % of GDP. However, earnings season just passed and next earnings cycle is months away; not a near-term catalyst.

screening.narrative: The Nasdaq suffered its worst day since April 2025 on Friday, plummeting 4.18% (-1,121 points) after a blowout NFP report (172K vs 85K expected) triggered a hawkish repricing of Fed expectations and a massive Treasury yield spike. Long-duration growth stocks, especially mega-cap tech and semiconductors, collapsed as real yields jumped; equities are now pricing in higher-for-longer rates rather than 2026 cuts. The selloff was amplified by announced or pending dilutive equity raises from Google, Meta, and possibly Microsoft to fund AI capex, plus broad conviction exits from AI/momentum trades. This is no longer a fresh reaction—it is an extended, multi-hour rout that has already burned speculative longs and repositioned risk.
catalysts_ahead: CPI (June 2026 data release) — will reset inflation and rate-cut expectations
FOMC Meeting (if scheduled) — Fed guidance on rate path critical
Apple WWDC (timing unclear but noted as upcoming) — Tim Cook's AI legacy and valuation stress test
NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, META earnings guidance — forward capex and cloud growth commentary
Treasury auctions and 10Y yield stabilization/trend break
screening.risk_warning: If NFP data proves transient or June inflation rolls over sharply, rate-cut bets resurface and yields plummet, invalidating the bearish thesis. Additionally, if mega-cap Tech (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL) report blowout earnings + raise guidance meaningfully despite capex concerns, multiple compression could reverse. Oversold conditions and VXN extremes could trigger a 2–3% counter-bounce; do not assume straight-down continuation.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is in PHASE 3 (extended down move, -4%+ on the day, intraday lows tested). Shorts are extended. Wait for stabilization around 28,500–28,700 support before adding to shorts; consider long scalps on any pop back to 29,200–29,400 with tight stops. Avoid chasing the down move—risk/reward is asymmetric now.

factors.name: NFP & Fed Rate Hike Expectations
factors.summary: May NFP printed 172K vs 85K expected with upward revisions to prior months (per multiple sources); KPMG economist noted bond market now pricing 25bp rate hike in 2026, representing hawkish shift. This is catastrophic for long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Surge
factors.summary: US Treasury yields rocketed across the curve post-NFP, with 2-year up 12bps and 10-year up 6bps. Higher nominal yields compress NQ valuations dramatically given extreme duration profile of mega-cap tech.

factors.name: Real Yields (implied)
factors.summary: Strong jobs data with wages lagging inflation suggests Fed staying restrictive longer; real yields rising is poison for growth stocks trading at 25-40x forward earnings.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Funding Pressure
factors.summary: Meta considering raising tens of billions via stock offering (FT report) following Google's $85B equity sale this week; Alphabet capex guidance to $190B (double 2025). Massive dilution risk and signal that AI spend is cannibalizing shareholder value.

factors.name: Semiconductor Sector Collapse
factors.summary: Chip stocks suffered worst day in 6 years per MarketWatch; Marvell and Micron shares tumbled despite Marvell joining S&P 500. Headline: 'Wall Street ends sharply lower as chips slide' — semiconductors are 15%+ of NQ weight.

factors.name: Nasdaq Price Action
factors.summary: NQ down 1,121 points (biggest point drop on record), -4.18% session decline, worst day since April 2025. $1.8 trillion S&P wipeout. Technical damage severe: momentum broken.

factors.name: Bitcoin Correlation Breakdown
factors.summary: BTC crashed below $60K to October 2024 lows; crypto selling pressure mirrors tech risk-off. Historically NQ-BTC correlation spikes during deleveraging episodes — both getting hit simultaneously confirms broader risk liquidation.

factors.name: Rate Cut Expectations Evaporate
factors.summary: Citigroup maintaining lonely 3-cut call while market has flipped to pricing hikes; hot jobs report swept aside possibility of cuts 'anytime soon' per CNBC. Zero rate relief for tech multiples in 2026.

factors.name: Meta Stock Specific
factors.summary: META down 6.56% to $586 on equity raise fears; stock down 11% YTD after only +12.7% in 2025. AI capex arms race forcing shareholder dilution — negative signal for entire hyperscaler complex.

factors.name: Retail Capitulation Risk
factors.summary: Bloomberg headline: 'Tech Selloff, Bitcoin Drop Test Retail Investor Strength' — the reliable bid from retail army that supported markets for years is now being tested. If retail capitulates, no natural buyer left.

factors.name: Goldman Buying Opportunity Call
factors.summary: Goldman's Flood sees pullback as buying opportunity with S&P 500 path to 8,000 this year. However, this is a contrarian fade signal given extended AI rally and valuation stretch.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risks
factors.summary: US attacks Iranian sites after drone launches; heightened Middle East tensions and Red Sea choke point vulnerabilities add risk premium. Though not direct tech impact, raises macro uncertainty.

factors.name: VIX / Volatility Surge (implied)
factors.summary: Record point drop in NQ and S&P wipeout implies VIX spike and dealer positioning shift; negative gamma environment means accelerated selling into weakness.

factors.name: ETF Flows (implied)
factors.summary: QQQ likely seeing heavy redemptions given -4.18% NQ decline; systematic deleveraging and trend-following algos likely adding to selling pressure.

factors.name: Apple WWDC Ahead
factors.summary: WWDC next week with Tim Cook's AI legacy on the line per CNBC; high-stakes event but binary outcome. Market unlikely to position aggressively before clarity.

confirmation.narrative: Nasdaq is experiencing a violent deleveraging triggered by blowout May NFP (172K vs 85K exp) that has flipped Fed expectations from cuts to potential hikes, sending yields surging and crushing long-duration tech multiples. The -4.18% session plunge marks the worst day since April 2025, with semiconductors suffering their worst decline in 6 years. Crucially, mega-cap AI funding models are breaking: Meta exploring tens of billions in equity raises following Google's $85B sale, signaling the AI capex arms race is cannibalizing shareholder value. With retail strength now tested, rate relief evaporated, and Bitcoin correlation confirming broad risk-off, the 2-year tech momentum trade faces structural headwinds.
catalysts_ahead: Apple WWDC (next week) - AI strategy unveiling, high stakes for Tim Cook's legacy
CPI release (next scheduled date) - any inflation surprise will compound Fed hike fears
FOMC decision (next meeting) - market now pricing 25bp hike possibility in 2026
Meta/Alphabet equity offering details - dilution magnitude will set tone for sector
Semiconductor earnings guidance - sector health critical after 6-year worst decline
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if Fed officials push back against hike pricing in next 48-72h, or if yields reverse sharply lower (10Y back below 4.2%). Goldman's institutional buying call could mark capitulation low if coordinated bid emerges. Apple WWDC surprise could trigger short-term momentum reversal in mega-cap tech.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already 4.18% down and in Phase 2; avoid chasing here. Wait for relief rally retest of 29,200-29,500 resistance zone (broken support) or bear flag formation before adding shorts. Alternatively, if breaks 28,500 decisively, wait for backtest of that level.

screening.narrative: Friday's blowout NFP (172K jobs, +revisions) triggered a capitulation sell-off in Nasdaq (-4.18% on the day, down -1.47% over 4h, -2.73% over 24h). Real yields spiked, Fed rate-hike expectations re-priced higher, and mega-cap tech faced dual headwinds: (1) soaring long-duration discount rates crushing valuation multiples, and (2) massive dilutive equity offerings from Google, Meta, Microsoft signaling AI-capex desperation and ROI uncertainty. The semiconductor sector posted its worst day in 6 years, and crypto contagion (BTC sub-$60K) accelerated equities lower. The move is now extended into PHASE_3, with profit-taking likely dominating. Prior analyst consensus (SELL 7-8/10) validates the downside; key question is whether we see a technical retest bounce or further capitulation.

screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended into PHASE_3; avoid chasing shorts. Wait for NDX to retest 29,200–29,500 resistance or for a 1–2% intra-day bounce off current lows (28,500–28,800 support zone) before re-engaging short positions. If support breaks with volume, target 27,500–28,000. For longs, risk/reward poor until at least 24–48h consolidation or dovish catalyst emerges.

factors.name: China Tech Restrictions
factors.summary: China tightening oversight of private investment funds; China/HK users unable to access SpaceX website. Continued regulatory friction in key growth market for US tech.

confirmation.narrative: The blowout NFP report (172K vs 85K expected) has triggered a violent repricing: markets now expect Fed rate hikes instead of cuts in 2026, sending Treasury yields soaring and crushing long-duration Nasdaq valuations. The tech selloff is broad and deep—chip stocks having their worst day in 6 years, Meta down 6.5% on dilution fears from potential equity raise, and Bitcoin breaking $60K in sympathy. This is a regime shift: the labor market strength that seemed benign last week now looks like fuel for Fed tightening, and mega-caps raising tens of billions in equity (Google $85B, Meta considering similar) signals stretched valuations and an AI capex arms race that investors are suddenly unwilling to fund at current multiples.

screening.narrative: NAS100 entered a sharp corrective phase on Friday following a blowout US jobs report (172K vs. 85K expected) that extinguished rate-cut expectations and repriced Fed hikes into 2026. This triggered a cascade across long-duration tech: nominal and real yields spiked, mega-cap hyperscalers announced massive capex needs and equity dilution plans (Google $85B capex, Meta/Microsoft fundraising), and semiconductor stocks collapsed on demand concerns. The Nasdaq Composite suffered its largest one-day point drop on record (-1,121 points, -4.18%), wiping $1 trillion from the sector. Crypto followthrough (Bitcoin <$60K) confirms risk-off sentiment. The 2-day move (-2.73% in 24h) is now in PHASE_2: move is well underway and participants are rotating out of growth names.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: US Dollar rallied to ~100.10 on Friday after hot NFP (FXStreet 2026-06-05T21:22), benefiting from higher rate expectations. Nasdaq has significant international revenue exposure; mild headwind but not a dominant driver today given domestic catalyst dominance.

factors.name: VIX / Volatility Regime Shift
factors.summary: Record Nasdaq point drop and broad-based selloff typical of volatility spike regimes. Options dealers likely short gamma in mega-caps after two-month rally; rebalancing selling could amplify downside into any relief bounce.

factors.name: Prior Bias Consistency
factors.summary: Bias history shows SELL7/10→SELL9/10 across three reads (June 6, 02:58 → 06:32 → 09:26 UTC). This is a sustained, multi-cycle bearish call, gaining credibility as price action confirms with -2.73% 24h drop.

factors.name: Geopolitical / Supply-Chain Risks
factors.summary: Iran-US tensions and screwworm border issues present tail risks but not immediate catalysts for further Nasdaq repricing. Fed tightening cycle is the primary driver; geopolitics secondary.

key_levels.support: 28,500 (4h lows near here), 28,000 (psychological), 27,500 (recent swing low zone)

screening.narrative: Friday's hot NFP (172K vs. 85K) triggered a regime shift from "Fed cuts priced in" to "Fed will hike in 2026." Treasury yields shot higher, compressing long-duration tech multiples and forcing mega-caps (Google, Meta) to announce massive equity raises and capex cycles. This sparked a cascading loss of confidence in AI ROI and prompted broad de-risking across Nasdaq (down 4.18% Friday, worst day since April 2025). Overnight and into today, the selloff has persisted: -1.47% in 4h, -2.73% in 24h. The move is in PHASE_2—initial catalyst fresh, downside momentum building, but not yet fully extended. Micro-cap damage (crypto, small-cap tech) and mega-cap dilution signals suggest institutional flows are turning defensive. Prior SELL9/10 calls from earlier in the week are holding and gaining conviction.
catalysts_ahead: CPI / Core CPI release (if due within 48-72h) — could flip bias if soft, extending the selloff if hot
FOMC meeting (if scheduled soon) — Chair Warsh's first hawkish or dovish signal will reset Nasdaq repricing
Mega-cap earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, META, AMZN) — forward guidance on capex and cloud demand will be scrutinized for signs of AI ROI disappointment
Fed speakers & rate-hike confirmation (Warsh, Powell if available) — any hint of Q3 or Q4 2026 hike would extend current selloff
Treasury 2-10 curve (watch for further steepening) — if 10y stays elevated, duration headwind for Nasdaq persists
Options expiry / quadruple witching (if within window) — gamma-driven rallies may offer tactical bounce but secondary to trend
screening.risk_warning: If CPI print comes in cooler-than-expected (below 2.8% core), or if Fed official signals pause/dovishness, Treasury yields could reverse sharply lower, invalidating this bearish bias and triggering a snap-back rally of 2-3% in Nasdaq. Additionally, if mega-cap earnings (esp. NVDA, MSFT) beat and guide strongly on cloud/AI demand, market could re-rate capex spending as necessary, ending the selloff. Lastly, if VIX spiked extremely (>25), panic selling could be followed by aggressive short-covering and institutional bottom-fishing, creating a sharp intraday bounce that traps fresh shorts.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 (underway, not extended yet). SELL bias is intact, but best tactical entry is either on a pullback/relief bounce to 29,200-29,500 resistance, or on fresh breakdown below 28,500 support with confirmed break of 28,000. Current price (28,957) is mid-range; chasing further downside in fast markets is high-risk. Wait for either a higher-low bounce (then short into retest of resistance) or a clean break of structural support on high volume.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Move in progress but not fully extended; fresh shorts on any rally toward 29,200-29,400 resistance, or chase current levels with tight risk given strong fundamental backdrop.

factors.name: NFP blowout & Fed rate hike expectations
factors.summary: May NFP printed 172K vs 85K expected (per news), sparking bond market pricing of 25bp hike in 2026 (Bloomberg). KPMG economist noted 'bond market pricing reflects expectations of a 25 basis point rate hike in 2026.' This is catastrophic for long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: 10-year Treasury yield surge
factors.summary: US Treasury yields 'skyrocket across the whole curve,' with 2-year up 12bp and 10-year up 6bp (FXStreet). Rising nominal yields compress growth equity valuations, especially tech at 25-30x forward earnings.

factors.name: Real yields rising (TIPS)
factors.summary: Strong jobs data + higher nominal yields = rising real yields, which are kryptonite for long-duration Nasdaq stocks. No direct TIPS data cited but implied by nominal yield surge and declining inflation expectations.

factors.name: Mega-cap equity dilution fears
factors.summary: Meta considering 'raising tens of billions of dollars in a stock offering' to fund AI (FT/Investing.com), stock down -6.56% to $581. Alphabet just completed record $85B equity raise. Massive dilution overhang hitting sentiment across FAAMG complex.

factors.name: Tech sector capitulation
factors.summary: NASDAQ down -4.18% Friday, 'worst trading day since April 2025,' with session lows near -4.5%. S&P 500 down -2.65%. MarketWatch reports '$1.8 trillion wipeout' and 'Nasdaq tallies biggest point drop on record.' Breadth is collapsing.

factors.name: AI capex concerns & model routing
factors.summary: CNBC reports 'model routing is a fix for AI overspending. That's a problem for OpenAI and Anthropic.' Companies shifting away from most expensive models, undermining hyperscaler capex narrative that drove 2025-2026 rally.

factors.name: Crypto correlation breakdown
factors.summary: Bitcoin cracked $60K, 'lowest level since October 2024' (CNBC), with $1.6B in liquidations. Crypto-tech correlation intact on risk-off days; selling pressure confirming broader tech de-risking.

factors.name: Semiconductor stock weakness
factors.summary: Micron stock 'fell 13% on Friday' (news headline). 'Semiconductor pullback weighs on KRW' (DBS). Chip stocks are NDX bellwethers; this weakness is structural, not rotational.

factors.name: Goldman buying call dismissed
factors.summary: Goldman's Flood sees 'buying opportunity' and 'clear path for S&P 500 to 8,000' (Bloomberg), but this is a classic sell-side permabull take. Market is ignoring it; price action trumps commentary.

factors.name: VIX/VXN spike
factors.summary: While specific VIX level not cited, -4.18% NASDAQ day implies VXN (Nasdaq vol) spiked hard. Dealer gamma likely flipping negative, amplifying downside momentum.

factors.name: Dollar strength
factors.summary: US Dollar rallied to 'near 100.10 from daily low of 99.16' post-NFP (FXStreet). NDX components derive ~40-60% revenue internationally; stronger dollar = FX headwinds and multiple compression.

factors.name: Iran conflict escalation
factors.summary: 'US attacks Iranian sites after Iran launches drones' (news). Geopolitical risk + oil price pressure = stagflation fears, negative for growth equities.

factors.name: Retail sentiment cracking
factors.summary: Bloomberg: 'Tech Selloff, Bitcoin Drop Test Retail Investor Strength Ahead of SpaceX IPO.' The retail bid that supported 2025-2026 rally is wavering; SpaceX IPO may pull liquidity from secondary markets.

factors.name: ETF flows (anticipated)
factors.summary: No QQQ/XLK flow data in news, but -4.18% NASDAQ day likely triggers systematic de-risking, CTA trend-following sells, and vol-control fund deleveraging into next week.

confirmation.narrative: The Nasdaq is experiencing a violent repricing driven by a triple threat: 1) Blowout NFP data (172K vs 85K exp) has bond markets pricing a 2026 Fed hike, sending 10-year yields surging 6bp and real yields higher—kryptonite for long-duration tech; 2) Mega-cap equity dilution fears as Meta follows Alphabet in tapping markets for tens of billions to fund AI capex, raising questions about ROIC and capital discipline; 3) Breakdown in the AI narrative as 'model routing' adoption suggests hyperscaler capex may have overshot demand. The -4.18% NASDAQ drop (worst since April 2025) confirms this is not a dip-buy but a regime shift. Retail strength is cracking, crypto correlation is confirming risk-off, and semiconductors are leading lower. Goldman's bullish call is being ignored by the market.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Chair Warsh comments (MarketWatch noted 'key to upcoming Fed meeting' is how Warsh reacts to rate hike hints)
CPI data (next major inflation print will confirm/deny rate hike pricing)
Meta equity offering details (timing and size will drive sentiment)
SpaceX IPO pricing June 9-10 (could drain liquidity from secondary markets)
Any Fed speaker commentary on NFP reaction
China-Taiwan tensions (chip export controls mentioned in news)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: 1) Fed speakers push back hard on rate hike expectations, causing yields to reverse; 2) Mega-cap tech (MSFT, NVDA, AAPL) report blowout earnings with strong AI demand commentary in coming weeks; 3) 10-year yield fails to hold above 4.20-4.30% range and reverses lower; 4) Major geopolitical de-escalation (Iran) sparks risk-on rotation back into growth. Given Phase 2 status (move in progress, -2.73% in 24h), a technical short-squeeze back to 29,200-29,400 is possible but should be sold.

factors.name: NFP Blowout & Fed Rate Hike Repricing
factors.summary: May NFP came in at 172K vs 85K expected (Bloomberg, 2026-06-05), sparking sharp repricing of Fed rate hike odds for 2026. Higher nominal yields compress long-duration growth multiples—Nasdaq's primary vulnerability.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Spiking
factors.summary: 10-year yields surged post-NFP, with gold and silver tanking ~8-10% (FXStreet, 2026-06-05). Real yields likely also rising, crushing tech multiple expansion—the core driver of Nasdaq's recent rally.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Capex Shock & Equity Dilution
factors.summary: Meta considering tens of billions in equity raise (Bloomberg, 2026-06-05), Google already sold $85B in equity, Microsoft eyeing capital markets. Dilution and AI capex inflation are pressuring sentiment on hyperscalers (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, META all in NDX).

factors.name: Semiconductor Weakness & Zcash Privacy Bug
factors.summary: Micron fell 13% Friday (MarketWatch), and semiconductor rally 'hit a wall' per Bloomberg (2026-06-05). Zcash bug disclosure triggered crypto selloff ($200B wiped, Bitcoin sub-$60K)—risk-off mood spreads to correlated growth assets.

factors.name: VIX & Volatility Surge
factors.summary: Nasdaq's worst day since April 2025 (down 4.18% Friday). VIX 'finally caught up' per Bloomberg headline, volatility regime shifting from complacency to uncertainty. Tech-heavy drawdown (1,121-point Nasdaq point drop) suggests capitulation risk.

factors.name: Tariff & Trade War Escalation
factors.summary: Trump administration proposing new tariffs on 60 nations citing forced labor (Bloomberg, 2026-06-06); Iran war ongoing, shipping costs spiking 109% since conflict (Bloomberg). Tech supply chains and margin outlook deteriorating.

factors.name: Dollar Strength Persisting
factors.summary: USD rallied to ~100.10 post-NFP (FXStreet, 2026-06-05), supporting higher yields. Nasdaq components have ~40-50% international revenue; stronger dollar is headwind for earnings translation.

factors.name: Prior Bias Continuity
factors.summary: SELL bias repeated three times (2026-06-06 06:32, 09:26, 11:26) at 7-9/10 conviction. This is Phase 2 of a sustained downtrend, not a reversal—confluence suggests persistence.

key_levels.support: 28,500 (Friday's session low ~25,753 on Nasdaq composite, NAS100 equiv ~28,800-29,000)

screening.narrative: The Nasdaq was crushed Friday on the back of a blowout May NFP (172K vs 85K consensus), igniting Fed rate hike repricing and Treasury yields surging. Long-duration tech—the core of the NDX—is extremely sensitive to rising real yields, and the simultaneous capex shock from Meta, Google, and Microsoft (equity dilution + AI cost inflation) has triggered a confidence break. Semiconductor weakness (Micron -13%, NVDA pressure) and risk-off sentiment (Bitcoin sub-$60K, crypto volatility spilling into equities) reinforce the selling. This is Phase 2 of a downtrend, not a bounce attempt; the move is extended but momentum remains bearish.
catalysts_ahead: Apple WWDC keynote Monday (2026-06-09) – AI strategy and guidance critical for confidence
Fed Chair Warsh policy signals and communication changes
CPI/PCE inflation data (if scheduled this week) – could reaffirm or soften rate hike odds
Mega-cap earnings (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL, NVDA capex guidance) – forward guidance on cloud/AI spend
Treasury yield direction – any further spike in 10Y will extend Nasdaq weakness
Iran/geopolitical headlines – shipping, tariff, energy supply shocks
screening.risk_warning: If Fed officials signal dovishness or a pivot away from rate hikes, or if inflation data cools sharply in the next 48h, yields could reverse lower and trigger a fast short-squeeze rally in tech. Additionally, a geopolitical de-escalation (Iran peace deal) could soothe inflation fears and reverse the tariff/supply-chain narrative.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended (down 2.73% in 24h, 4h down 1.47%, likely in intraday bounce phase). Wait for retest of 29,200–29,400 resistance before shorting; or enter on break of 28,500 support with tight stop at 29,000. Do not chase lower—watch for capitulation reversal signals (extreme VXN, options positioning flow) before covering shorts.

factors.name: NFP blowout & rate hike expectations

factors.name: Semiconductor sector reversal
factors.summary: Chip stocks hit a wall Friday per Reuters 'crash up' reversal article. Micron stock fell 13% Friday (MarketWatch). Monster rally in semiconductors finally reversed, triggering VIX spike.

factors.name: VIX breakout
factors.summary: Wall Street's 'fear gauge' punched back per Reuters, finally catching up with other volatility metrics as chip stock rally reversed. Elevated vol suppresses tech multiples.

factors.name: AI capex concerns & model routing
factors.summary: Model routing trend (CNBC article) threatens OpenAI/Anthropic revenue as companies shift away from running everything on most powerful (expensive) models. Questions emerging on AI spending ROI sustainability.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk (Iran war, tariffs)
factors.summary: Iran war entering fourth month with oil inventories low (MarketWatch). Trump administration proposing new tariffs on 60 nations over forced labor (Bloomberg). Container rates spiked 109% since Iran war started.

factors.name: New Fed Chair Warsh uncertainty
factors.summary: MarketWatch notes upcoming Fed meeting key is 'how Warsh reacts to hints of rate hike.' New chair may make swift communication changes, adding policy uncertainty during hawkish pivot.

factors.name: Technical breakdown
factors.summary: NAS100 down -2.73% in 24h, -1.47% in 4h. NASDAQ hit new session lows into close at 25,753. Momentum clearly bearish with selling accelerating on funding news.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO capital rotation risk
factors.summary: SpaceX preparing biggest public debut in history with multitrillion-dollar hype (MarketWatch). CoinDesk asks if retail selling Bitcoin to buy SpaceX IPO. Potential capital rotation away from existing tech.

factors.name: Dollar strength
factors.summary: USD rallied to near 100.10 from 99.16 low post-NFP (FXStreet). NDX components have heavy international revenue exposure; stronger dollar headwind to earnings.

confirmation.narrative: The SELL bias from prior sessions has violently validated. Friday's NFP blowout (172K vs 85K) triggered aggressive Fed rate-hike repricing, sending yields surging and crushing long-duration tech multiples. NASDAQ recorded its largest point drop ever (-1,121 points), with the semiconductor rally finally reversing. The selloff accelerated late session on reports that Meta and Alphabet are tapping equity markets for tens of billions to fund AI capex, raising dilution fears and ROI questions. Risk-off cascade evident across all assets: Bitcoin sub-$60K, $1.8T equity wipeout, VIX spike. Move is Phase 2 (well underway but not yet exhausted).

Fed meeting with new Chair Warsh (MarketWatch: key is his reaction to rate hike hints)
CPI/inflation data next release

Semiconductor earnings guidance updates
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidates if Fed pushes back hard against rate hike expectations, yields reverse sharply lower, or mega-cap tech companies provide surprisingly strong AI monetization updates at WWDC/upcoming events. Oversold bounce risk exists given extended move, but underlying drivers (yields, dilution, AI ROI questions) remain intact.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move is Phase 2—selloff underway but not exhausted. Look for bear-flag consolidation or retest of 29,200-29,500 resistance zone before adding shorts; avoid chasing at current levels after -2.73% drop.

factors.name: Hot NFP + Fed Rate Hike Repricing

factors.name: Real Yields & Long-Duration Sensitivity
factors.summary: With nominal yields spiking and inflation expectations rising (war in Iran continuing ~100 days, container shipping rates up 109% per Bloomberg 2026-06-06T12:06), real yields likely moved higher, directly pressuring the long-duration tech complex that constitutes ~50% of NAS100.

factors.summary: Micron fell 13% Friday per MarketWatch (2026-06-05T11:06). Chip stocks had rallied hard but VIX finally caught up as the 'crash up' in semiconductors reversed per MarketWatch (2026-06-06T12:58), signaling exhaustion in the narrow AI/chip rally.

factors.name: Broadening Risk-Off Sentiment
factors.summary: NASDAQ fell 4.18% (worst day since April 2025) while S&P 500 down 2.65% per Investinglive (2026-06-05T20:40), indicating breadth deterioration and rotation out of growth. Gold sinking, dollar rallying, volatility spiking—classic risk-off.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran War at 100 Days)
factors.summary: War in Iran approaching 100-day mark with no resolution (Bloomberg 2026-06-06T15:02), US crude inventories perilously low (MarketWatch 2026-06-05T12:00), and shipping costs surging—backdrop of supply shocks and inflation persistence weighs on growth outlook.

factors.name: Crypto Weakness (Risk Proxy)

factors.name: Analyst-Macro Divergence
factors.summary: Bloomberg Markets (2026-06-06T13:00) notes stock analysts remain bullish post-earnings but macro soothsayers see 'vastly different markets'—classic sign of hidden fragility and divergence before broader selloff.

factors.name: Extended Price Move (24h: -2.73%, 4h: -1.47%)
factors.summary: NASDAQ already down 2.73% in 24h; move is in PHASE 3 territory. Friday's 4.18% plunge suggests selling momentum may continue early week, but late chasers should await pullback/retest rather than fresh shorts.

key_levels.resistance: 29,400 (Friday high before final dump), 29,800 (prior weekly resistance)

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in a sustained SELL phase triggered by Friday's blowout 172K NFP print vs 85K expected, which repriced Fed rate hike expectations sharply upward and spiked 10-year yields. This compresses the already-expensive long-duration growth multiples that tech dominates. Mega-cap tech capex announcements (Microsoft, Meta, Alphabet raising capital or signaling massive spend) have spooked investors on dilution and capital intensity concerns. The semiconductor rally that powered NQ upside in recent weeks has finally rolled over (Micron -13%, chip stocks reversing), and crypto weakness (BTC sub-$60K) confirms the risk-off mood. Geopolitical backdrop (Iran war, oil supply strain) adds inflation persistence risk. The move is already extended (down 2.73% in 24h), placing us in late-phase territory.

Wait for retest of 29,200–29,400 zone on any bounces, or for a daily close above 29,000 to signal potential short-squeeze before considering re-entry on weakness. factors.name: NFP & Fed Rate Hike Expectations

factors.summary: 4h change -1.47%, 24h change -2.73%. Move is already 3+ hours extended in downward direction. Historical PHASE_3 condition typical of near-term pullback/consolidation risk despite ongoing bearish bias.

factors.name: Sustained SELL Bias Continuation
factors.summary: Your prior reads (2026-06-06 11:26, 13:29, 15:15) all flagged SELL 7-9/10. This is NOT a fresh reversal—bias is sustained, lending credibility to the bear case but also suggesting move may be maturing.

key_levels.support: 28,500–28,600 (near-term structural support; test likely as Phase 3 extends)
key_levels.resistance: 29,200–29,400 (recent highs before Friday rout; pullback target if oversold bounce occurs)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught in a structural downtrend triggered by a blowout US jobs report (172K NFP vs 85K expected) that forced Treasury yields and real rates sharply higher on 2026-06-05. Long-duration tech multiples are compressing as the market reprices Fed rate-hike odds for 2026. Semiconductor stocks—a crucial NDX component—hit their worst day in 6 years, and mega-cap AI narratives are fracturing as capex disillusionment sets in (Uber/MSFT/Nvidia budget blowouts, hiring reversals). The 'June swoon' is materializing as the index reverses from all-time highs and geopolitical risk (Iran war inflation) reinforces the case for higher rates. Your prior SELL bias (SELL 7-9/10) remains intact.
catalysts_ahead: CPI/Core CPI release (if scheduled within 24–72h)—inflation surprise would reset rate trajectory
FOMC minutes or Fed speaker commentary re: rate path in 2026
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL earnings (if imminent)—forward capex guidance is now under scrutiny
Oracle earnings next week (mentioned in MarketWatch as potential rally catalyst, but skepticism is high)
China trade/tariff developments (forced labor tariff proposals cited)
Further semiconductor supply-chain news (TSMC, ASML, Samsung capacity/demand updates)
screening.risk_warning: If CPI/PCE comes in cooler than expected, or Fed signals patience on rate hikes, the bear case collapses and yields roll over, favoring a sharp NDX reversal. Additionally, a strong earnings surprise from NVDA, MSFT, or AAPL could reignite AI/growth momentum and flip bias back to BUY. Oversold conditions (VIX, positioning) could trigger a sharp squeeze higher if sentiment swings. Move is already extended (PHASE_3); a pullback to 28,500–28,700 is likely before any further downside.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended in PHASE_3—do not chase lower. Wait for pullback/bounce to 29,100–29,200 resistance to consider fresh short entries, or re-assess after a definitive break below 28,500 support on lower volume (suggesting exhaustion). Monitor CPI/Fed speak for potential bias flip.

factors.name: NFP blowout + rate hike expectations
factors.summary: May NFP came in at 172K vs 85K expected (MarketWatch), triggering Fed rate-hike repricing. Markets now pricing potential rate increases in 2026, toxic for long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Treasury yields surge
factors.summary: 10Y yields spiked sharply on NFP surprise, causing growth stock multiple compression. Real yields rising = brutal for Nasdaq's high-duration names.

factors.name: Semiconductor sector collapse
factors.summary: Marvell, Micron shares tumbled as chip sector suffered 'worst day in 6 years' (MarketWatch). NASDAQ down 4.18%, worst session since April 2025, driven by semiconductor selloff.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech funding pressure
factors.summary: Meta reportedly considering raising 'tens of billions' in stock offering (FT), Alphabet seeking fresh capital with capex up to $190B. Dilution fears and financing costs rising as rates reprice higher.

factors.name: AI budget blowouts backfiring
factors.summary: Uber blew entire 2026 AI budget by April, now hiring humans because 'workers are cheaper' (MarketWatch). Questions emerging about AI ROI and capex sustainability at hyperscalers.

factors.name: June swoon technical setup
factors.summary: MarketWatch warns 'don't rule out June swoon—S&P 500 pushing its limits.' Friday's avalanche of selling crushed stocks from record highs earlier in week. VIX spiking after lagging.

factors.name: Crypto correlation breakdown
factors.summary: Bitcoin crashed below $60K with $1.6B in liquidations, now erasing all post-Trump-election gains. Risk-off sentiment spreading across speculative tech and crypto simultaneously.

factors.name: Fed's Barr hawkish warning
factors.summary: Fed Vice Chair Barr warned 'weakening bank rules courts financial instability' in speech. Regulatory tightening + rate hike path = double headwind for growth stocks.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk (Iran war day 100)
factors.summary: Iran conflict approaching 100-day mark with missile attacks in Persian Gulf. Elevated oil prices, supply chain disruption, inflation risk keeping Fed hawkish—all negative for tech multiples.

factors.name: Earnings season optimism fading
factors.summary: MarketWatch notes 'even upbeat Oracle earnings next week might not be enough to rally market.' Strongest earnings season in years unable to offset macro repricing—classic late-cycle signal.

confirmation.narrative: The NFP blowout (172K vs 85K expected) triggered violent repricing of Fed rate-hike odds, sending Treasury yields surging and crushing Nasdaq's duration-sensitive tech stocks. Semiconductors suffered their worst day in 6 years while mega-caps face dilution fears (Meta, Alphabet capital raises) and AI ROI questions (Uber's budget blowout). The -4.18% Nasdaq plunge represents Phase 2 of a macro regime shift from 'AI goldilocks' to 'rates matter again.' Bitcoin's collapse below $60K confirms broad risk-off across speculative assets.
catalysts_ahead: Oracle earnings (week of June 9)
CPI report (next scheduled release)
Any Fed speaker commentary on rate path post-NFP
Semiconductor earnings guidance updates
Mega-cap capex/funding announcements
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidates if: (1) Fed officials push back aggressively on rate-hike repricing, (2) 10Y yields reverse sharply lower, (3) major hyperscaler announces blowout AI revenue/margin beat changing ROI narrative, (4) technical reclaim above 29,500 with volume.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already 4.18% down intraday and -2.73% over 24h—Phase 2 underway. Wait for retest rally toward 29,200-29,500 resistance zone before adding shorts, or use break below 28,500 as confirmation for continuation.
